Headline: Red Line Trains bypassing North/Clybourn Station
ShortDescription: Red Line trains are running, but not stopping at North/Clybourn due to flooding.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Red Line trains are running, but not stopping at North/Clybourn due to flooding.

As a result of the delay, shuttle buses are available to provide connecting service from Fullerton to Clark/Division in both directions.

At this time, also consider other service alternatives that may serve your trip, such as nearby bus routes, or using buses to other rail lines.

These may include:

#8 Halsted

#72 North

We apologize for this inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.

Headline: Green and Pink Line Service Delayed at Ashland/Lake
ShortDescription: Green and Pink Line trains are standing at Ashland/Lake due to signal problems. Crews working to restore service.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Green and Pink Line trains are standing at Ashland/Lake due to signal problems.

Shuttle buses have been requested from to provide connecting service in both directions for:

Green Line Riders -Harlem/Lake to Clark/Lake

At this time, also consider other service alternatives that may serve your trip, such as nearby bus routes or using buses to other rail lines.

These may include:

#9 Ashland
#21 Cermak
#49 Western
#50 Damen

Personnel are working to restore service. Allow extra travel time.

Headline: Service Between 54th/Cermak and Polk, then Racine (Blue Line) Stations, Only
ShortDescription: Pink Line trains operate from 54th to Polk only, then connect w/Blue Line at Racine for continuing service, due to signal problems at Ashland/Lake.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Pink Line service has been temporarily suspended between Polk and the Loop due signal problems at Ashland/Lake.

As a result, Pink Line trains are running as follows:

  • Between 54th/Cermak and Polk, and then are diverted to Racine on the Blue Line for direct connections at Racine to continuing Blue Line train service to/from downtown.

Using connecting Blue Line service:

  • For service to downtown from Pink Line stations between 54th/Cermak and Polk, take a Pink Line train toward the Loop and stay on board until the train reaches Racine on the Blue Line. Transfer to an O'Hare-bound Blue Line train from the same platform for service into downtown.

  • For service from downtown to Pink Line stations between Polk and 54th/Cermak, take a Blue Line train toward Forest Park to Racine. Stay on the same platform side and wait for a 54th/Cermak-bound Pink Line train.

Other possible alternatives may include: 

  • #9 Ashland
  • #21 Cermak
  • #49 Western
  • #50 Damen
  • #52 Kedzie/California
  • #53 Pulaski
  • #54 Cicero
  • #60 Blue Island-26th
  • #82 Kimball-Homan
  • #94 South California
  • #157 Streeterville-Taylor buses

...including to/from and in combination with other 'L' services.

Note that if you take a bus to/from the Pink Line, you may also be able to connect to/from the Blue Line's Forest Park branch or Green Line along Lake Street directly via that bus route.

See station personnel for further assistance, and pay attention to announcements on trains and at stations for service information.

We are working to restore service as quickly as possible. Allow extra travel time.

Headline: #60 Blue Island/26th Temporary Westbound Reroute near Western/Blue Island
ShortDescription: Westbound 60 Blue Island/26th buses are temp. rerouted via Western, Cermak, California and 26th, over regular route: due to, flooding near Western/Blue Island.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Westbound #60 Blue Island/26th buses are temporarily rerouted via Western, Cermak, California and 26th, over regular route: due to, flooding near Western/Blue Island.

Eastbound buses are not affected.

Allow extra travel time.
